affordances.name: Motivation architecture diagnosis
affordances.mechanism: When performance or commitment is weak despite nominal capability, require the answer to diagnose autonomy, competence support, relatedness, standards, and feedback quality before prescribing pressure, rewards, or vague purpose.

use_when: The apparent performance problem may be a mismatch in autonomy, competence support, or relatedness.
Compliance exists but ownership, initiative, retention, or creativity remain low.
Leaders can change decision latitude, feedback quality, growth path, or social belonging.
do_not_use_when: The problem is routine task compliance where simple incentives and standards are sufficient.
Autonomy language would hide missing standards, quality bars, or accountability.
The case lacks ability to redesign conditions and only motivational slogans are available.
case_evidence_needed: Where motivation, commitment, or initiative is failing.
Current standards, feedback quality, and accountability structure.
What environmental condition can actually be redesigned.

treatment_requirements.description: Require the answer to inspect autonomy, competence, and relatedness conditions before adding pressure or rewards.
good_output_shape: Names which SDT need is likely thwarted.
Adds a condition redesign rather than just pressure.
Warns against purpose or autonomy slogans without structure.

diagnostic_questions: Is the bottleneck autonomy, competence, relatedness, or a missing standard?
Are people able to do the work but not internalizing ownership?
What condition can be redesigned rather than merely incentivized?
misuse_guards: Do not reduce motivation to reward size alone.
Do not invoke autonomy while leaving expectations or quality bars vague.
Do not use purpose language to suppress criticism or skeptical doubts.

source_evidence.source_quote: When these three needs are satisfied, individuals are motivated, productive, and happy
motivation architecture is the real bottleneck
Danger When Autonomy Talk Masks Missing Standards

review_notes.normalization_note: Batch 14 outcome: strong_affordance_record. The source supports SDT as motivation architecture diagnosis across autonomy, competence, and relatedness.
dropped_material: Did not extract generic purpose or empowerment slogans.
Did not treat motivation as reward size alone.
open_questions: Later packet review should test whether this card improves team, learning, and behavior-change cases.

absence_records.reason: The source contrasts SDT with external carrots and sticks and frames motivation around autonomy, competence, and relatedness.

source_evidence.section_hint: Most Powerful Analogy: The Third Drive

absence_records.reason: The source treats autonomy as one of three needs and warns that autonomy talk can mask missing standards.

source_evidence.section_hint: Most Powerful Analogy: The Third Drive

source_evidence.source_quote: compliance is possible but commitment is weak

review_notes.normalization_note: Batch 14 source reading found strong reviewed depth for motivation architecture, not motivational decoration.
dropped_material: No runtime, prompt, lane, packet, deterministic pressure, or user-facing change is implied.
open_questions: Later packet review should check whether SDT helps distinguish motivation, capability, and standards gaps.
